name: Holo Quantum
summary: Unmodified PennyLane running natively in Hologram's κ-addressable substrate as a topological quantum computing emulator. Paste any PennyLane script and run it verbatim on the tqc.anyon device.

description.p: A real quantum-computing workbench for Hologram. PennyLane — the standard library for quantum machine learning — is installed and run completely unmodified inside the κ-substrate (CPython compiled to WebAssembly via Pyodide). Your existing PennyLane scripts run verbatim.

Circuits execute on the first-party tqc.anyon device. Its exact-state core delegates every amplitude to PennyLane's own reference statevector engine, so results match default.qubit bit-for-bit. On top, the substrate adds topological (D(Z6) anyonic) braiding semantics and κ-collapse: equivalent circuits reduce to a single content address and return instantly, provably (the TQC 'echo' property, applied to circuits).

Honesty over marketing: this is an emulator. Abelian D(Z6) braiding is not universal on its own — universality here comes from the exact statevector core, not from the braiding. The topology buys provable identity and instant repeats, not the computation itself.

ul: Exact results — matches default.qubit to floating-point tolerance
κ-collapse: equivalent circuits return instantly from one content address
No server, no install, no CDN — 100% your hardware (Law L4)

releases.description: First slice: unmodified PennyLane 0.45.1 on the in-substrate Pyodide; first-party tqc.anyon device (exact statevector core via PennyLane's reference simulator + TQC κ-collapse memo) registered by entry point so qml.device("tqc.anyon") resolves by name. Verbatim-script REPL with κ-cache inspector.
